blockingIssues.description: TestDoRequestWithToken_Headers claims to verify request headers (Authorization, User-Agent, Accept) are set correctly, but headerCheckTransport.RoundTrip() does not validate the expectedHeaders field against actual request headers. It returns a success response unconditionally. The test passes but doesn't verify the headers it claims to verify.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add guidance that test helper functions called from production code should be defined in production files, not test files. If validateFFmpegArgs is needed in production, it should live in ffmpeg.go, not ffmpeg_test.go.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: test-coverage-downloader: validateFFmpegArgs defined in ffmpeg_test.go but called from ffmpeg.go production code

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add guidance to review secret scanning false positive patterns. Test data like []byte containing numeric strings ('1234567890123456') commonly triggers Droid-Shield false positives and should be documented as exempt.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: test-coverage-downloader handoff noted Droid-Shield false positive on []byte test data in TestDecryptChunkErrorPaths

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify the skill procedure for test-coverage-only tasks. Step 5 mentions starting local API on port 8080 for manual verification, but this is unnecessary for pure test coverage improvements. Workers should use unit tests with httptest for coverage tasks without API behavior changes.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: test-coverage-server: Worker correctly used httptest instead of manual API verification and met all verification criteria
